title: Tanker report - Week 14
summary:
VLCC
Charterers maintained their vice-like grip on owners, with rates for 270,000mt falling 8.5 points to WS 37.5, while a newbuilding to Korea agreed WS 31.5 basis 280,000mt. Going west, rates for 280,000mt to the US Gulf are assessed three points lower at around WS 19, Cape/Cape, with an options cargo achieving slightly more. In West Africa, rates for 260,000mt fell four points with both WS 40 and WS41 agreed for China discharge.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: Charterers maintained their vice-like grip on owners, with rates for 270,000mt falling 8.5 points to WS 37.5, while a newbuilding to Korea agreed WS 31.5 basis 280,000mt. Going west, rates for 280,000mt to the US Gulf are assessed three points lower at around WS 19, Cape/Cape, with an options cargo achieving slightly more.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: West Africa rates for 130,000mt to Europe fell briefly to WS 50, before improved volumes of enquiry led to firmer sentiment. Petroineos paid WS 57.5 from Escravos, and there is talk of WS 60 having been done.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, rates for 80,000mt from Ceyhan are now seen at WS 85, although lower was done. In Primorsk - with ice restrictions - the market for 100,000mt peaked at WS 75, before easing to WS 65 for straight UKContinent discharge.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: Rates for 75,000mt Middle East Gulf/Japan eased five points to WS 97.5, while the 55,000mt trade fell 12.5 points to WS 100. The market for 37,000mt Continent/USAC lost 25 points to WS 145.
